title: "Failed to connect to bus" error when running systemd enabled container rootless  (podman 2.0.5)
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run -d --privileged --name test --systemd=true ubi8/ubi-init /sbin/init`

2. `podman exec -it test bash -c "systemctl status"`


**Describe the results you received:**
And I get the error:
```
Failed to connect to bus: No such file or directory
```

If I run as root, it works:
```
$ sudo podman run -d --privileged --name test --systemd=true ubi8/ubi-init /sbin/init
0a792fca966d80f56326a98ee98d9dd35de86e7155f19a43272765fed6d715b4
$ sudo podman exec -it test bash -c "systemctl status"
● 0a792fca966d
    State: running
     Jobs: 0 queued
   Failed: 0 units
    Since: Wed 2021-01-13 16:53:28 UTC; 11s ago
   CGroup: /machine.slice/libpod-0a792fca966d80f56326a98ee98d9dd35de86e7155f19a43272765fed6d715b4.scope
           ├─30 systemctl status
           ├─36 more
           ├─init.scope
           │ └─1 /sbin/init
           └─system.slice
             ├─systemd-journald.service
             │ └─21 /usr/lib/systemd/systemd-journald
             └─dbus.service
               └─27 /usr/bin/dbus-daemon --system --address=systemd: --nofork --nopidfile --systemd-activation --syslog-only


```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Expect podman rootless to work with systemd containers.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.5
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.7
Built:        Thu Sep 24 17:58:13 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.15.1
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.20-2.module_el8.3.0+475+c50ce30b.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.20, commit: 1019ecdeda3936be22162bb1cca308192145de53'
  cpus: 4
  distribution:
    distribution: '"centos"'
    version: "8"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: localhost
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 4.18.0-240.1.1.el8_3.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 5441921024
  memTotal: 8044417024
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: runc-1.0.0-68.rc92.module_el8.3.0+475+c50ce30b.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.2-dev'
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.1.4-2.module_el8.3.0+475+c50ce30b.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.4
      commit: b66ffa8e262507e37fca689822d23430f3357fe8
      libslirp: 4.3.1
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
  swapFree: 0
  swapTotal: 0
  uptime: 23h 11m 59.95s (Approximately 0.96 days)
registries:
  search:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.redhat.io
  - docker.io
store:
  configFile: /home/centos/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 1
    paused: 0
    running: 1
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: fuse-overlayfs-1.1.2-3.module_el8.3.0+507+aa0970ae.x86_64
      Version: |-
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.1.0
        FUSE library version 3.2.1
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.26
  graphRoot: /home/centos/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 4
  runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers
  volumePath: /home/centos/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes

  APIVersion: 1
  Built: 1600970293

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-2.0.5-5.module_el8.3.0+512+b3b58dca.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Latest versions on Fedora 33 work, but I need it on CentOS 8.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Selinux is permissive.

comments.body:
I've just tried it on CentOS 8 and it works without `--privileged`.

Any reason for using `--privileged`?  I think that can confuse systemd as it runs with more capabilities than needed.

Does it make any difference if you drop it?
No need to run with --privileged.  Should avoid --privileged almost always.

@giuseppe tried and it seems same:
```
$ podman run -d --name test --systemd=true  registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8/ubi-init /sbin/init
$ podman exec -it test bash -c "systemctl status"
Failed to connect to bus: No such file or directory
Error: exec session exited with non-zero exit code 1: OCI runtime error
```

I've tried with podman 2.1.1 and it works on CentOS 8 using the commands above as rootless.

Is there systemd running after the first command?

> I've tried with podman 2.1.1 and it works on CentOS 8 using the commands above as rootless.
> 
> Is there systemd running after the first command?

Yes, I compiled podman 2.1.1 and it worked for me as well. Even 2.0.6 version works like a charm. But the problem is that we can use only podman version provided by CentOS 8 and it's 2.0.5. And this version does not work.
I'm not sure how to check if systemd is running, all systemd related commands fails with "bus" error.
```
$ ./podman-2.0.5 exec -it test bash
[root@98e8348ec425 /]# ps uax
USER         PID %CPU %MEM    VSZ   RSS TTY      STAT START   TIME COMMAND
root           1  0.5  0.0  93640  7748 ?        Ss   22:47   0:00 /sbin/init
root          11 20.0  0.0  19212  3672 pts/0    Ss   22:48   0:00 bash
root          22  0.0  0.0  51820  3732 pts/0    R+   22:48   0:00 ps uax
[root@98e8348ec425 /]# pidof systemd
1
```

OK, by "build and test" way I've found the commit that fixed this problem, it's https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/4da3677e3f243de1cedca11976f156f980e132dc
this line:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/2b7793b6121d336a285fb7b9a7612c221cbf63d2/pkg/domain/infra/abi/system.go#L80

Can you come with some workaround here before this fix?

you could wrap the command with `systemd-run --scope --user`:

`systemd-run --scope --user podman run -d --name test --systemd=true ubi8/ubi-init /sbin/init`

does it work better?

@giuseppe yes, with this command it runs fine. The problem is, we use it in [molecule podman driver](https://github.com/ansible-community/molecule-podman) and there is no option to prefix such command, it can take only `podman` command options.
Seems like no choice but downgrade to 1.6.4.
can you run the entire wrapper program inside of `systemd-run --scope --user`?
In any case, I am going to close the issue as it is not affecting upstream.  Feel free to comment further though

I am getting this same issue (identical in fact) using podman 3.0.2.

Has there been a regression ?
Should I open another issue or keep it contained within this issue ?

I observe the same behaviour on Leap 15.2. I guess the reason is, that `cgroupManager` is `cgroupfs` and not `systemd`. 

On the host I have the following version:
```
$ podman version
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.13.15
Built:        Tue Jan  5 13:00:00 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
With cgroupv1 and runc. When I run the following as rootless, I get the same as @sshnaidm 
```
$ podman run -d --name test ubi8/ubi-init /sbin/init
$ podman exec -it test bash -c "systemctl status"
Failed to connect to bus: No such file or directory
```
It makes no difference, if I add `--privileged` or `--systemd=true`.
But if I run the same commands on the same host as root, it works. The difference is that the `cgroupManager` is `systemd` in root mode. 
On a virtual machine with debian 10, it also works. There I use podman 3.0.1 with cgroupv2 and systemd as `cgroupManager`.
Can we make rootless use systemd as the cgroupManager? That may solve this all in one go?

> Can we make rootless use systemd as the cgroupManager?

Add `systemd.unified_cgroup_hierarchy=1` to your kernel parameters of your host system. Check it out how do you have to do it with your os and bootloader. 

>  That may solve this all in one go?

I really don't know if it this is the reason, but for me it seems to be..
